contract_responses.source: Section 5.1: 'We will pay the death benefit to the beneficiary if: (a) The insured dies while this policy is in force, and (b) We receive proof of death acceptable to us, and (c) The death occurs before the policy expiry date.'
Section 8.1: 'Upon receipt of proof of death, we will pay the beneficiary: Face Amount + Paid-Up Additions - Policy Loans - Loan Interest'
Article 5.1: 'OPTION A (Level Death Benefit) - Death Benefit = Specified Amount'; Article 9 contains no exclusion for natural causes; policy is in force with no outstanding loans.
Section 4.2: 'OPTION II - Variable Death Benefit (CURRENTLY SELECTED) Death Benefit = Face Amount + Account Value'; no exclusion applies to natural causes after 2-year suicide/war window.
Section 2: After Year 2 (on or after May 1, 2026): 'We pay the full benefit amount ($10,000)'; 5 years post-issue exceeds the 2-year graded benefit period.
Section 1.3: 'Your coverage is subject to the terms, conditions, and exclusions in the Master Policy and this certificate.' No exclusion applies to natural causes; death benefit payable.
Section 1.1: 'We will pay the death benefit if the insured dies while this policy is in force and all premiums have been paid.' Natural causes after 5 years, no exclusion applies.

Article 1.2: 'The death benefit is payable only upon the death of the SECOND insured to die (survivorship basis).' This is a joint policy; only one death occurs in this scenario — no benefit is triggered.
Section 2.1: 'If the insured dies during the policy term while the policy is in force, we will pay $300,000 to the beneficiary.' All premiums paid, natural causes, 5 years in.

Article 6.1: 'OPTION 1 - Level Death Benefit (CURRENT) Death Benefit = $500,000 (specified amount)'; Article 11 contains no exclusion applicable to natural causes death after the 2-year contestability window.
Section 7.1: 'SUICIDE CLAUSE: If the insured commits suicide within two years from the issue date, our liability is limited to a refund of premiums paid.' Death at 10 months is within 2 years.
Section 11.1: 'SUICIDE: If the insured dies by suicide within two years from the issue date, we will pay only the premiums paid, without interest.' 10 months is within 2 years.
Article 9.1: 'SUICIDE EXCLUSION: If insured dies by suicide within 24 months of issue date, we pay only the Account Value, without interest.' 10 months is within 24 months.
Section 8.1: 'SUICIDE: If death occurs by suicide within 2 years of issue or reinstatement, we pay only premiums paid minus withdrawals.' 10 months is within 2 years.
Section 5.1: 'SUICIDE: If death occurs by suicide within 2 years of the issue date, we will only return the premiums paid.' 10 months is within 2 years.
Section 8.1: 'SUICIDE: If death occurs by suicide within 12 months of the coverage effective date, we will not pay a death benefit.' 10 months is within 12 months — no benefit payable.
Section 1.3: 'EXCLUSION - SUICIDE: If death occurs by suicide within 2 years of the issue date, we will pay only the premiums paid, without interest.' 10 months is within 2 years.
Article 7.1: 'SUICIDE CLAUSE: If either insured commits suicide within 2 years of the issue date, the death benefit is limited to return of premiums paid.' 10 months is within 2 years.

Section 7.1: 'SUICIDE: If the insured dies by suicide within 24 months of the issue date, we will pay only the premiums paid without interest. No death benefit is payable.' 10 months is within 24 months.

Article 11.1: 'SUICIDE: If insured commits suicide within 2 years of issue or reinstatement, we pay only premiums paid minus any withdrawals and charges, without interest.' 10 months is within 2 years.
Section 7.1: 'If the insured commits suicide within two years from the issue date, our liability is limited to a refund of premiums paid.' 13 months is within 2 years.
Section 11.1: 'If the insured dies by suicide within two years from the issue date, we will pay only the premiums paid, without interest.' 13 months is within 2 years.
Article 9.1: 'If insured dies by suicide within 24 months of issue date, we pay only the Account Value, without interest.' 13 months is within 24 months.
Section 8.1: 'If death occurs by suicide within 2 years of issue or reinstatement, we pay only premiums paid minus withdrawals.' 13 months is within 2 years.
Section 5.1: 'If death occurs by suicide within 2 years of the issue date, we will only return the premiums paid.' 13 months is within 2 years.

Section 8.1: 'SUICIDE: If death occurs by suicide within 12 months of the coverage effective date, we will not pay a death benefit.' 13 months is BEYOND 12 months; therefore the suicide exclusion no longer applies and the death benefit is payable.
Section 1.3: 'If death occurs by suicide within 2 years of the issue date, we will pay only the premiums paid, without interest.' 13 months is within 2 years.
Article 7.1: 'If either insured commits suicide within 2 years of the issue date, the death benefit is limited to return of premiums paid.' 13 months is within 2 years.
Section 7.1: 'If the insured dies by suicide within 24 months of the issue date, we will pay only the premiums paid without interest.' 13 months is within 24 months.
Article 11.1: 'If insured commits suicide within 2 years of issue or reinstatement, we pay only premiums paid minus any withdrawals and charges, without interest.' 13 months is within 2 years.
Section 5.1: Death benefit payable if insured dies while policy is in force. Suicide exclusion only applies within 2 years (Section 7.1). At 14 months, natural illness is not excluded.
Section 8.1: 'Upon receipt of proof of death, we will pay the beneficiary: Face Amount + Paid-Up Additions - Policy Loans - Loan Interest.' Natural illness at 14 months, no applicable exclusion.
Article 5.1: Death benefit = Specified Amount. No exclusion applies to natural illness. Contestability period (Article 9.2) allows contest only for material misrepresentation; no such issue here.
Section 4.2: Death benefit = Face Amount + Account Value under Option II. War exclusion (8.2) and aviation exclusion (8.3) do not apply. Natural illness at 14 months is covered.
Section 2: 'During Year 2 (May 1, 2025 - April 30, 2026): We pay 70% of face amount ($7,000) plus all premiums paid.' 14 months falls in Year 2 of this graded benefit policy.
Section 8.1: Suicide exclusion only applies within 12 months. Section 8.2: 'PRE-EXISTING CONDITION LIMITATION: None.' Natural illness at 14 months is fully covered.
Section 1.1: Death benefit payable if insured dies while policy is in force and all premiums paid. No exclusion applies to natural illness after 12 months.
Article 1.2: 'The death benefit is payable only upon the death of the SECOND insured to die (survivorship basis).' Single insured death does not trigger death benefit on this joint policy.
Section 2.1: Death benefit of $300,000 payable if insured dies during the policy term. No exclusion applicable to natural illness. Section 3.3 also provides proportional premium refund.
Article 6.1: Death benefit = $500,000. No exclusion applicable to natural illness. Contestability under Article 11.2 is for material misrepresentation only, not applicable here.
Section 5.1: Death benefit payable if insured dies while policy is in force. Accidental death within grace/active period and no exclusion applies to accidents.
Section 8.1: Full death benefit payable. No aviation/war/accident exclusions exist in C2. Death within 2-year suicide window does not apply to accidental death.
Article 5.1: Death benefit = Specified Amount. Article 9.1 suicide exclusion does not apply to accidental death. Policy is in force at 8 months.
Section 8 lists exclusions: suicide (within 2 years), war exclusion (within 24 months), and aviation (as pilot/crew, unless declared). An accidental fall does not fall under any of these exclusions.

Section 2: 'EXCEPTION - ACCIDENTAL DEATH: If death results from an accident at any time, we pay the full benefit amount immediately, plus all premiums paid.' Death from accidental fall within 90 days = full $10,000.
Section 8.1 suicide exclusion applies only within 12 months. An accidental death at 8 months from a fall is not suicide. Full death benefit payable.
Section 1.1: Death benefit payable while policy is in force. Rider 5 (Accidental Death Benefit) also pays an additional $750,000 if death results from accidental injury within 90 days of accident.
Article 1.2: Death benefit payable only upon the second insured's death. A single insured's accidental death does not trigger the survivorship benefit.

Section 2.1 and 3.3: Full death benefit payable plus proportional premium refund. Section 7.3: 'NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, travel, or hazardous activity exclusions apply.' Accidental death is covered.
Article 6.1: Death benefit = $500,000. Article 11 contains only suicide and contestability limitations; accidental death from a fall is fully covered.
C1 is a single-life term policy on John Michael Anderson. Joint survivorship provisions do not exist in this contract.
C2 is a single-life whole life policy on Margaret Rose Chen. No joint survivorship provisions.
C3 is a single-life universal life policy on Robert James Wilson. No joint survivorship provisions.
C4 is a single-life variable universal life policy on Dr. Amanda Katherine Foster. No joint survivorship provisions.
C5 is a single-life final expense policy on Dorothy Mae Johnson. No joint survivorship provisions.
C6 is a group term certificate for Alex Morgan Taylor. No joint survivorship provisions.
C7 is a single-life term policy on Christopher David Bennett. No joint survivorship provisions.

Article 2.1: 'Upon the death of the second insured, we will pay $2,500,000 to the designated beneficiary, minus any outstanding loans.' Both insureds have died with no outstanding loans; full benefit is payable.
C9 is a single-life return of premium term policy on Nicole Christine Martinez. No joint survivorship provisions.
C10 is a single-life indexed universal life policy on Jessica Lynn Thompson. No joint survivorship provisions.
C1 (SecureLife term policy) does not mention a free look or cancellation provision. No free look clause is stated in this contract.
Section 12: 'FREE LOOK PERIOD: You may cancel this policy within 30 days of receipt for a full refund of premiums paid.' 20 days is within 30 days.
C3 does not contain a free look period provision. No such clause is mentioned in the contract.
C4 does not contain a free look period provision within its stated sections.
Section 7.1: '30-DAY FREE LOOK: You can cancel within 30 days of receiving this policy for a full refund.' 20 days is within 30 days.
C6 is a group certificate; it does not contain a free look period provision.
C7 does not explicitly state a free look period in its provisions.
Article XI: 'The policy owner may cancel within 30 days of policy receipt for a full refund of premium paid.' 20 days is within 30 days.
C9 does not contain a free look period provision within its stated sections.
C10 does not contain a free look period provision within its stated sections.
Section 6.3: 'If the premium is not paid by the end of the grace period, the policy will lapse and coverage will terminate.' C1 is a term policy with no cash value or non-forfeiture options.
Section 9.4: 'If no election is made within 60 days of lapse, Extended Term Insurance will automatically apply.' The full face amount continues for a limited period based on cash value.
Article 10.3: 'Upon lapse, any Account Value in excess of surrender charges will be paid to you automatically.' The remaining account value (after charges) is returned; coverage ends.
Section 7.1: Policy lapses after 61 consecutive days of insufficient account value. Any remaining surrender value is returned per Section 7.3 reinstatement provisions.
Section 8.1: 'After 2 years, if you stop paying premiums, your policy will automatically continue with a reduced benefit amount based on the cash value. No further premiums needed.'
Section 4.1(e): Coverage terminates on 'the last day for which required premiums have been paid.' Group term has no non-forfeiture options.
Section 3.2: 'GRACE PERIOD: 31 days from due date.' After grace period, the policy lapses. No non-forfeiture options are stated for this term policy.

Article 4.6: Grace period of 60 days applies. If premium not received the policy cancels, but no automatic non-forfeiture default is mentioned. The accumulated cash value remains accessible via loan per Article V.
Section 4.4: 'If premium is not paid within grace period, policy lapses and return of premium benefit is forfeited.' ROP term policies have no non-forfeiture option.
Article 10.3: 'If premium not received within grace period, policy lapses and any remaining surrender value is paid to you.' Remaining account value (minus surrender charges) is returned.
Section 8.1: 'You may convert this policy to a permanent life insurance policy without evidence of insurability.' Conversion must occur before expiry or insured's 65th birthday.
C2 is already a permanent whole life policy. Conversion from term to permanent does not apply.
C3 is already a permanent universal life policy. Conversion from term does not apply.
C4 is already a permanent variable universal life policy. Term-to-permanent conversion does not apply.
C5 is already a permanent whole life (guaranteed issue) policy. Term conversion does not apply.

Section 5.1-5.4: 'If your group coverage terminates for any reason except non-payment of premium, you may convert to an individual whole life policy without evidence of insurability.' Must apply within 31 days.

Section 4.2: 'You may convert all or part of this policy to permanent life insurance without evidence of insurability: Before the policy expiration date, or before your 70th birthday, whichever is earlier.'
C8 is a joint survivorship whole life policy — already permanent. Term-to-permanent conversion does not apply.
Section 5.1: 'You may convert this policy to a permanent life insurance policy without evidence of insurability.' Available before policy expires or before insured's 65th birthday.
C10 is already a permanent indexed universal life policy. Term-to-permanent conversion does not apply.
C1 (term life) does not contain a reinstatement provision. Lapse results in termination per Section 6.3.

Section 10.1: 'You may reinstate this policy within 3 years of lapse if: (a) You provide evidence of insurability satisfactory to us (b) You pay all overdue premiums with interest at 6% per annum (c) You repay or reinstate any policy loans with interest.' 18 months is within 3 years.
C3 does not contain a formal reinstatement provision. Article 10 addresses lapse and grace period but no reinstatement option is described.
Section 7.3: 'Reinstatement within 3 years requires: Evidence of insurability; Payment of all back charges plus 6% interest; Repayment of outstanding loans.' 18 months is within 3 years.
Section 8.3: 'You may restart your policy within 2 years by paying missed premiums with interest.' 18 months is within 2 years.
C6 does not contain a reinstatement provision. Group coverage terminates per Section 4.1 without a reinstatement option.
C7 does not explicitly contain a reinstatement provision in its stated sections.
C8 does not contain a reinstatement provision in its stated articles.

Section 8.1: 'If the policy lapses, you may apply for reinstatement within 5 years if: You provide satisfactory evidence of insurability; You pay all past due premiums with interest at 6% per annum; The insured is still alive.' 18 months is within 5 years.
C10 does not explicitly contain a reinstatement provision in its stated articles.
Section 6.2: 'If a premium is not paid by the due date, a grace period of 31 days is allowed. Coverage continues during the grace period.' Death within the 31-day grace period is covered.
Implied by standard whole life policy terms; Section 8.1 states death benefit is paid upon proof of death. Grace period provisions (similar to C1) ensure coverage during the grace period.

Article 10.1: 'If Account Value is insufficient to pay monthly deductions, we will notify you. Policy continues for 61 days.' For flexible-premium policies, if account value covers charges, coverage continues regardless of planned premium.
Section 7.1: Policy lapses only after 61 consecutive days of insufficient account value. If account value is sufficient, death within 20 days of a missed payment is covered.
Section 3.4: 'GRACE PERIOD: If payment is not received, you have 30 days to pay before the policy cancels.' Death within 20 days of a missed payment is within grace period — coverage continues.

Section 4.1(e): Coverage ends on 'the last day for which required premiums have been paid.' For employer-paid basic coverage, this scenario depends on employer payment; for voluntary, 20-day grace is implied.
Section 3.2: 'GRACE PERIOD: 31 days from due date. Coverage continues during grace period. If death occurs during grace period, unpaid premium will be deducted from death benefit.'
Article 4.6: 'GRACE PERIOD: If payment is not received, you have 60 days to pay before the policy cancels.' Death 20 days after missed premium is within the 60-day grace period.
Section 4.4: 'Grace period: 31 days. If premium is not paid within grace period, policy lapses.' Death 20 days after missed due date is within the 31-day grace period — coverage continues.
Article 10.1-10.2: 'Grace period: 61 days to pay sufficient premium to bring policy back in force.' Death within 20 days of a missed payment is within the grace period.
C1 is a term life policy. It has no cash value and no policy loan provision.
Section 7.1: 'You may borrow against the cash value after the policy has been in force for one full year.' Policy at just over 1 year is eligible. Maximum: 90% of current cash surrender value.
Article 7.1: 'Maximum loan: 90% of current Account Value.' No minimum time requirement specified — loans available as long as sufficient account value exists.
Section 6.1: 'POLICY LOANS: Maximum: 90% of surrender value.' No minimum time requirement stated; available once surrender value exists.
Section 4.3: 'You can borrow against the cash value or surrender the policy for the cash value at any time.' However, at 1 year, cash value may be minimal per the schedule.
C6 is a group term life certificate with no cash value accumulation and no policy loan provision.
C7 is a term life policy with no cash value accumulation. No policy loan provision exists.
Article 5.3: 'POLICY LOANS: Available after policy has been in force for 2 years.' Policy at just over 1 year does NOT yet qualify — loan is not yet available. Status is DENIED.
Section 6.1: 'Policy loans are NOT available under this policy type.' ROP term does not permit policy loans.
Article 7.1: 'POLICY LOANS: Available after 1 year.' Policy is just over 1 year, so loans are available. Maximum: 90% of surrender value.
C1 is a term life policy with no cash value. Partial withdrawals are not applicable.
C2 (whole life) does not provide for partial withdrawals; access to cash value is via policy loans (Section 7) or surrender (Section 9), not withdrawals.
Article 6.1-6.2: 'You may make partial withdrawals from Account Value at any time. Minimum withdrawal: $500.' A $2,000 withdrawal exceeds the $500 minimum and is permitted.

Section 6.2: 'PARTIAL WITHDRAWALS: Minimum: $1,000; Maximum: Account value minus $5,000; Processing fee: $50.' A $2,000 withdrawal meets the minimum and is permitted (subject to surrender charges if within Year 7).
C5 mentions borrowing against or surrendering for cash value but does not provide a partial withdrawal feature. Section 4.3 covers loans and surrender only.
C6 is a group term life certificate with no cash value or withdrawal provisions.
C7 is a term life policy with no cash value or partial withdrawal provision.
Article 5.4: 'WITHDRAWALS: Partial withdrawals are not permitted under this policy type.'
Section 6.2: 'Partial withdrawals are NOT permitted.' Section 6.3: only full surrender or waiting until end of term for ROP payment.
Article 7.2: 'PARTIAL WITHDRAWALS: Minimum: $500.' A $2,000 withdrawal meets the minimum. First 10 withdrawals are free; $50 fee thereafter.
Section 6.2-6.3: Premiums are due monthly. Grace period is 31 days; if unpaid, policy lapses. C1 does not allow premium skipping.

Section coverage summary: 'Annual Premium: $3,240, Payment Frequency: Annual.' C2 requires annual premium payments; skipping a month is effectively a lapse risk, though non-forfeiture options exist after 3 years.
Article 2.3: 'You may skip payments as long as the Account Value is sufficient to cover monthly deductions.' If account value is sufficient, skipping a payment month is explicitly permitted.
Section 7.1: Policy lapses only if account value is insufficient for 61 consecutive days. If account value covers monthly charges, skipping a premium is permissible.
C5 is a whole life policy with fixed monthly bank-draft premiums. Section 3.3 provides a retry on the 15th; skipping implies non-payment leading to grace period.
C6 is a group term policy where employer pays basic coverage. Employee voluntary premium is payroll-deducted; skipping is not a policy-owner-controlled option.
C7 is a term policy with fixed annual premiums. Skipping a payment triggers the grace period and potential lapse; flexible skipping is not a feature.
C8 has fixed annual premiums per Article IV. Skipping results in the grace period (Article 4.6). Flexible premium skipping is not a feature.
Section 4.3-4.4: 'CRITICAL: To receive the full return of premium at end of term, ALL premiums must be paid on time.' Skipping would cause lapse and forfeit ROP benefit.
Article 4.3: 'You may skip premium payments as long as account value is sufficient to cover monthly deductions.' If account value is sufficient, a skipped month is explicitly permitted.
C1 does not contain a waiver of premium rider or disability provision.
C2 does not include a waiver of premium for disability provision in the stated contract.
C3 does not include a waiver of premium or disability benefit provision.
C4 does not include a waiver of premium or disability benefit provision.
C5 does not include a waiver of premium or disability provision.

Section 11.1: 'If you become totally disabled before age 60 and remain disabled for 6 consecutive months, your voluntary life insurance premiums will be waived.' Age 55, 8 months of disability meets the criteria.

Rider 1 (Waiver of Premium Due to Disability): 'If you become totally disabled before age 60 and remain disabled for 6 consecutive months: All future premiums are waived. Coverage continues in full force.' Age 55, 8 months of disability qualifies.
Article 4.5: 'WAIVER OPTION: Premium waiver is not available on joint survivorship policies.'
C9 does not include a waiver of premium or disability benefit provision.
C10 does not include a waiver of premium or disability benefit provision in its stated articles.
C1 does not contain an accelerated death benefit or terminal illness provision.
C2 does not include an accelerated death benefit provision.
C3 does not include an accelerated death benefit provision.
C4 does not include an accelerated death benefit provision.
C5 does not include an accelerated death benefit provision.

Section 7.1: 'If you are diagnosed with a terminal illness with life expectancy of 12 months or less, you may request acceleration of up to 75% of your death benefit.' Life expectancy of 10 months qualifies.

Rider 2 (Accelerated Death Benefit - Terminal Illness): 'If diagnosed with a terminal illness: May access up to 95% of death benefit while living.' Policy defines terminal illness as 'expected to result in death within 24 months.' 10-month prognosis qualifies.
C8 does not include an accelerated death benefit provision.
C9 does not include an accelerated death benefit provision.
C10 does not include an accelerated death benefit provision.
C1 is a standard term policy with no return of premium feature. Upon surviving the term, coverage simply expires with no premium refund.
C2 is a whole life policy with no term expiry and no return of premium feature.
C3 is a universal life policy with no return of premium feature.
C4 is a variable universal life policy with no return of premium feature.
C5 is a permanent whole life policy with no term expiry or return of premium feature.
C6 is a group term certificate with no return of premium feature.
C7 is a standard 30-year term policy with no return of premium feature.
C8 is a permanent whole life joint policy with no return of premium feature.
Section 3.1: 'END OF TERM ROP: If the insured is alive on September 15, 2044, and all premiums have been paid, we will pay the policy owner $32,400 (100% of premiums paid over 20 years).'
C10 is an indexed universal life policy with no return of premium feature.
Section 7.2: 'No other exclusions apply to this policy.' No aviation exclusion is present in C1; death while operating a private aircraft is covered.
Section 11 lists only suicide and misstatement of age exclusions. No aviation exclusion exists in C2; death while operating a private aircraft is covered.
Article 9 lists only suicide and contestability limitations. No aviation exclusion exists; death while operating a private aircraft is covered.

Section 8.3: 'AVIATION: Death resulting from operating an aircraft as pilot or crew member is excluded unless declared and approved with extra premium.' No declaration or extra premium is mentioned for this policy.
Section 5.2: 'NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: This policy has no war exclusion, no aviation exclusion, and no travel restrictions.'
Section 8 only lists suicide and pre-existing condition (none) limitations. No aviation exclusion exists; death while operating a private aircraft is covered.

Section 6 (Aviation Exclusion Waiver): 'The standard aviation exclusion has been waived for this policy based on the insured's declared private pilot activities. Coverage includes death while operating or riding as a passenger in any aircraft.'
Article 7.4: 'NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, or travel exclusions apply to this policy.'
Section 7.3: 'NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, travel, or hazardous activity exclusions apply.'
Article 11 lists only suicide and contestability limitations. No aviation exclusion exists; death while operating a private aircraft is covered.
Section 7.2: 'No other exclusions apply to this policy.' No war exclusion exists; death in a military conflict is covered.
Section 11 contains only suicide and misstatement of age exclusions. No war exclusion exists in C2.
Article 9 contains only suicide and contestability provisions. No war exclusion exists in C3.
Section 8.2: 'WAR EXCLUSION: Death while serving in military conflict in a war zone is excluded if it occurs within 24 months of issue.' 18 months after issue is within 24 months.
Section 5.2: 'NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: This policy has no war exclusion, no aviation exclusion, and no travel restrictions.'
Section 8 lists only suicide limitation. No war exclusion exists in C6.

Section 6 (Aviation Exclusion Waiver) states aviation coverage includes death in any aircraft 'Exception: military combat operations.' However, this exception is only for the aviation rider, not the base policy. The base policy (Section 1) contains no war exclusion for the death benefit itself. Rider 5 (ADB) excludes war, but base death benefit is covered.
Article 7.4: 'NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, or travel exclusions apply to this policy.'
Section 7.3: 'NO OTHER EXCLUSIONS: No war, aviation, travel, or hazardous activity exclusions apply.'
Article 11 lists only suicide and contestability provisions. No war exclusion exists in C10.

Section 10.2: 'MISSTATEMENT OF AGE OR SEX: If the age or sex of the insured has been misstated, we will adjust the death benefit to what the premiums paid would have purchased at the correct age and sex.' Policy has been in force 3 years, beyond the 2-year contestability period.
Section 11.2: 'MISSTATEMENT OF AGE: If the insured's age was misstated, we will adjust the face amount to what the premium would have purchased at the correct age.' Policy in force 3 years.

Article 9.2 addresses contestability (material misrepresentation within 2 years). After 3 years, the contestability period has passed. Age misstatement results in benefit adjustment per standard insurance principles implied by the contract.
Section 8.1 and general industry practice: After the 2-year contestability period per Section 8.1, misstatement of age results in benefit adjustment, not denial. Policy in force 3 years.
C5 is a guaranteed issue policy with no medical questions and no age misstatement provision stated. Section 9.1 notes it was issued without medical questions.
C6 does not contain an explicit misstatement of age provision.
C7 does not contain an explicit misstatement of age provision in its stated sections.
C8 does not contain an explicit misstatement of age provision.
Section 9.4: 'MISSTATEMENT OF AGE: If age was misstated, we will adjust the death benefit to what the premium would have purchased at the correct age. ROP amount is not affected.'
C10 does not contain an explicit misstatement of age provision.
C1 is a term life policy with no cash value. There is no surrender value to pay upon cancellation.
Section 9.1: 'CASH SURRENDER: Receive the cash surrender value and terminate the policy.' Available after policy has been in force for three years. Year 3 qualifies.

Article 8.1: 'Cash Surrender Value = Account Value - Surrender Charge - Outstanding Loans.' Year 3 surrender charge is significant (Article 8.2: Year 1: 8%, interpolating Year 3 ~6%). Surrender is permitted.
Section 3.6: Surrender Charge in Year 3 is 5% of premium paid. Section 6.2 allows partial withdrawals. Full surrender terminates the policy and pays cash surrender value.
Section 4.3 and 8.2: 'You can borrow against the cash value or surrender the policy for the cash value at any time.' Cash value at Year 3 will be minimal but surrender is permitted.
C6 is group term life with no cash value. There is no surrender value available.
C7 is a term life policy with no cash value. There is no surrender value.
Article 10.1: 'The policy owner (the Trust) has all ownership rights including the right to... Surrender the policy.' Cash value has accumulated per Article 5.
Section 3.2: In Years 1-5, surrender yields 0% premium return. The policy can be surrendered, but no ROP refund is paid in Year 3. Section 10.1(d) confirms owner may surrender.
Article 8.1-8.3: 'Cash Surrender Value = Account Value - Surrender Charge - Outstanding Loans.' Year 3 surrender charge is 8% of premiums paid. Surrender is permitted with applicable charges.
C1 is a term life policy with a fixed death benefit only. No increasing or investment-linked death benefit option exists.
Section 8.1: Death benefit = 'Face Amount + Paid-Up Additions - Policy Loans - Loan Interest.' Paid-up additions accumulate from dividends, effectively increasing the death benefit.

Article 5.2: 'OPTION B (Increasing Death Benefit): Death Benefit = Specified Amount + Account Value.' Available by request. If selected, insured dying with substantial account value results in a higher death benefit.
Section 4.2: 'OPTION II - Variable Death Benefit (CURRENTLY SELECTED): Death Benefit = Face Amount + Account Value.' The full accumulated account value is included in the death benefit.
C5 is a fixed final expense policy. No increasing death benefit option exists.
C6 is a group term certificate with a fixed death benefit. No investment-linked or increasing death benefit option.
C7 is a term life policy with a fixed death benefit. No increasing or account-value-linked death benefit option.
C8 is a joint whole life policy with a fixed $2,500,000 death benefit, not an investment-linked product.
C9 is a return of premium term policy with a fixed death benefit. No investment-linked or increasing death benefit option.

Article 6.2: 'OPTION 2 - Increasing Death Benefit: Death Benefit = Specified Amount + Account Value.' Available by request; currently Option 1 is selected, but the scenario applies if Option 2 is chosen.
C1 is a fixed term life policy with no index-linked or investment component.
C2 is a participating whole life policy with guaranteed cash value growth. No index-linked component.
C3 is a universal life policy with a fixed/declared interest rate, not index-linked. Market index movement does not directly affect account value.

Section 5.1-5.2: 'The account value and death benefit (under Option II) are NOT guaranteed and will fluctuate based on sub-account performance. Investment risk is borne entirely by the policy owner.' A 15% index drop in equity sub-accounts would reduce account value proportionally.
C5 is a guaranteed issue whole life policy with no index or investment component.
C6 is a group term life certificate with no investment component.
C7 is a term life policy with no investment component.
C8 is a whole life joint policy with no index-linked component.
C9 is a return of premium term policy with no investment or index component.

Article 3.3: 'CRITICAL: You never lose money due to market decline. The worst case is 0% interest for that year.' Article 1.4: 'Floor Rate - The minimum interest rate (0%) that will be credited, protecting against market losses.' A 15% decline results in 0% credit, not a loss.
C1 is an already-issued policy. New applicants with health conditions would be subject to underwriting, but this contract does not address the application process for new applicants.
C2 is an already-issued policy. It does not describe the underwriting process for new applicants.
C3 is an already-issued policy. Underwriting for new applicants is not addressed.
C4 is an already-issued policy. Underwriting for new applicants is not addressed.

Section 1: 'There are no medical exams or health questions required.' Section 9.1: 'GUARANTEED ISSUE: This policy was issued without medical questions or exams.' An applicant with severe pre-existing conditions can obtain this policy.

Section 2.2: 'Basic coverage became effective automatically without requiring evidence of insurability.' Section 8.2: 'PRE-EXISTING CONDITION LIMITATION: None.' Group coverage accepts employees regardless of health.
C7 is an already-issued policy requiring preferred non-smoker underwriting class. Severely ill applicants would not qualify for this policy.

Section 10.1: 'INCONTESTABILITY: We cannot contest this policy after it has been in force during the insured's lifetime for two years from the issue date.' At 18 months, the contestability period has NOT expired; insurer may contest and deny based on material misrepresentation.

Section 13: 'We may contest this policy only within the first two years while the insured is living, and only for material misrepresentation in the application.' 18 months is within 2 years; omission of cancer diagnosis is material misrepresentation.
Article 9.2: 'CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material misrepresentation within 2 years of issue date while insured is living.' 18 months is within 2 years.
Section 8.1 (contestability implied): Within 2-year period, insurer may contest. Material misrepresentation (omitted cancer) during the contestability window permits denial.

Section 9.3: 'INCONTESTABILITY: After this policy has been in force for 2 years, we cannot cancel it or deny claims except for non-payment of premiums.' However, at 18 months, contestability applies. BUT Section 9.1 states it is guaranteed issue with no health questions — there can be no material misrepresentation on health. The claim would be paid.
Standard contestability applies to group certificates. 18 months is within the 2-year contestability period. Material misrepresentation may allow contest.

Section 5: 'We will not contest this policy after it has been in force for 2 years during the insured's lifetime, except for non-payment of premiums.' At 18 months, contestability period is still active; omitted cancer diagnosis is material.
Article 7.3: 'CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material misrepresentation within 2 years of issue while both insureds are living.' 18 months is within 2 years.
Section 7.2: 'MATERIAL MISREPRESENTATION: We may contest the policy within 2 years of issue if there was material misrepresentation in the application.' 18 months is within 2 years.
Article 11.2: 'CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material misrepresentation for 2 years from issue date.' 18 months is within 2 years.

Section 10.1: 'INCONTESTABILITY: We cannot contest this policy after it has been in force during the insured's lifetime for two years from the issue date.' Death at 23 months is within 2 years; insurer may contest and deny for material misrepresentation.

Section 13: 'We may contest this policy only within the first two years while the insured is living, and only for material misrepresentation in the application.' 23 months is within 2 years; false answers about foreign travel and license suspension are material misrepresentations.
Article 9.2: 'CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material misrepresentation within 2 years of issue date while insured is living.' 23 months is within 2 years.
Contestability within 2 years of issue is implied. 23 months is within 2 years; false travel and driving answers may constitute material misrepresentation.

Section 9.1: 'GUARANTEED ISSUE: This policy was issued without medical questions or exams.' No application health questions or travel/driving questions could have been answered falsely. Section 9.3: No misrepresentation basis for denial exists.
Standard contestability within 2 years applies to the group certificate. 23 months is within the 2-year contestability period; material misrepresentation may permit denial.

Section 5: 'We will not contest this policy after it has been in force for 2 years during the insured's lifetime, except for non-payment of premiums.' 23 months is within 2 years; misrepresentation about travel and driving may be material.
Article 7.3: 'CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material misrepresentation within 2 years of issue while both insureds are living.' 23 months is within 2 years.
Section 7.2: 'MATERIAL MISREPRESENTATION: We may contest the policy within 2 years of issue if there was material misrepresentation in the application.' 23 months is within 2 years.
Article 11.2: 'CONTESTABILITY: We may contest this policy for material misrepresentation for 2 years from issue date.' 23 months is within 2 years.
C1 is a fixed term life policy with no cash value accumulation or investment projection applicable.
C2 is a whole life policy with guaranteed cash value schedules, not a flexible-premium policy with variable projections based on assumed returns.
C3 is a universal life policy but the scenario specifies VUL assumptions (sub-accounts, 5% gross return). C3 credits fixed/declared interest rates.

Section 3 (Charges and Fees) and Section 2 (Investment Allocation): C4 is a Variable Universal Life policy. Projections require: 5% gross return on sub-accounts, less M&E charge of 0.90% (Section 3.2), administrative charge of $15/month (Section 3.3), COI charges based on face amount at risk (Section 3.4), fund expenses of 0.45%-1.25% (Section 3.5), and 5% premium load (Section 3.1). These are the contract maximum/current charges applicable for a 30-year projection.
C5 is a small final expense whole life policy, not a flexible-premium VUL investment product.

C7 is a term life policy with no cash value or investment projection.
C8 is a joint whole life policy with guaranteed cash value schedules, not a variable investment product.
